Question: A 3-Bus network is shown. Consider generators as ideal voltage sources. If rows 1, 2, and 3 of the \( Y_{\text{Bus}} \) matrix correspond to Bus 1, 2, and 3, respectively, then \( Y_{\text{Bus}} \) of the network is

Choices 
A. \(\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4j & j & j \\ j & -4j & j \\ j & j & -4j\end{array}\right]\) 
B. \(\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4j & 2j & 2j \\ 2j & -4j & 2j \\ 2j & 2j & -4j\end{array}\right]\) 
C. \(\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-\frac{3}{4}j & \frac{1}{4}j & \frac{1}{4}j \\ \frac{1}{4}j & -\frac{3}{4}j & \frac{1}{4}j \\ \frac{1}{4}j & \frac{1}{4}j & -\frac{3}{4}j\end{array}\right]\) 
D. \(\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-\frac{1}{2}j & \frac{1}{4}j & \frac{1}{4}j \\ \frac{1}{4}j & -\frac{1}{2}j & \frac{1}{4}j \\ \frac{1}{4}j & \frac{1}{4}j & -\frac{1}{2}j\end{array}\right]\)

normalized_choice_map.A: \(\left[\begin{array}{ccc}-4j & j & j \\ j & -4j & j \\ j & j & -4j\end{array}\right]\)

normalized_question_text:
Question: Assume that the threshold voltage of the N-channel MOSFET shown in is \( +0.75 \, \text{V} \). The output characteristics of the MOSFET are also shown. The transconductance of the MOSFET is:

Choices:
A. \( 0.75 \, \text{ms} \) 
B. \( 1 \, \text{ms} \) 
C. \( 2 \, \text{ms} \) 
D. \( 10 \, \text{ms} \)

Question: In the circuit shown in figure, the characteristic equation of the flip-flop is
Choices:
A. \( Q^{n+1} = S + \overline{R}Q^n \)
B. \( Q^{n+1} = \overline{S} + \overline{R}Q^n \)
C. \( Q^{n+1} = S + RQ^n \)
D. \( Q^{n+1} = \overline{S} + RQ^n \)

Question: Assuming the initial state of all flip-flops is 0, the flip-flop whose output voltage waveform is not 0 under the clock signal is ( ).
Choices
A. circuit A
B. circuit B
C. circuit C
D. circuit D

Question: The op-amp shown in the has a finite gain \( A = 1000 \) and an infinite input resistance. A step-voltage \( V_i = 1 \text{mV} \) is applied at the input at time \( t = 0 \) as shown. Assuming that the operational amplifier is not saturated, the time constant (in millisecond) of the output voltage \( V_o \) is _______. 
Choices 
A. 1001 
B. 101 
C. 11 
D. 1

Question: A transmission line terminates in two branches, each of length \(\lambda/4\), as shown. The branches are terminated by 50Ω loads. The lines are lossless and have the characteristic impedances shown. Determine the impedance \( Z_i \) as seen by the source. 
Choices: 
A. 200Ω 
B. 100Ω 
C. 50Ω 
D. 25Ω

Question: With S2 closed and S1 open, the current waveform in the delta winding will be ______.

Choices: 
A. a sinusoid at fundamental frequency 
B. flat-topped with third harmonic 
C. only third-harmonic 
D. none of these

Question: The output voltage \(\left(V_0\right)\) of the Schmitt trigger shown in swings between +15 V and -15 V. Assume that the operational amplifier is ideal. The output will change from +15 V to -15 V when the instantaneous value of the input sine wave is

Choices 
(A) 5 V in the positive slope only 
(B) 5 V in the negative slope only 
(C) 5 V in the positive and negative slopes 
(D) 3 V in the positive and negative slopes
normalized_answer_text: 5 V in the positive slope only

normalized_choice_map.A: 5 V in the positive slope only
normalized_choice_map.B: 5 V in the negative slope only
normalized_choice_map.C: 5 V in the positive and negative slopes
normalized_choice_map.D: 3 V in the positive and negative slopes

normalized_question_text:
Question: A 4:1 multiplexer is to be used for generating the output carry of a full adder. A and B are the bits to be added while \( C_{\text{in}} \) is the input carry and \( C_{\text{out}} \) is the output carry. A and B are to be used as the select bits with A being the more significant select bit.

Which one of the following statements correctly describes the choice of signals to be connected to the inputs \( I_0, I_1, I_2, \) and \( I_3 \) so that the output is \( C_{\text{out}} \)?

Choices: 
A. \( I_0 = 0 \), \( I_1 = C_{\text{in}} \), \( I_2 = C_{\text{in}} \), and \( I_3 = 1 \) 
B. \( I_0 = 1 \), \( I_1 = C_{\text{in}} \), \( I_2 = C_{\text{in}} \), and \( I_3 = 1 \) 
C. \( I_0 = C_{\text{in}} \), \( I_1 = 0 \), \( I_2 = 1 \), and \( I_3 = C_{\text{in}} \) 
D. \( I_0 = 0 \), \( I_1 = C_{\text{in}} \), \( I_2 = 1 \), and \( I_3 = C_{\text{in}} \)

Question: In the circuit shown in figure, if the waveform of the input terminal A and output terminal F of the NAND gate is as shown in figure, then this circuit is ( )
Choices
A. AND gate
B. OR gate
C. NOT gate

Question: Given the nonlinear system as shown in , determine the amplitude and frequency of the system's output \( c(t) \) during self-oscillation.

Choices: 
A. The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 8 \). 
B. The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 10 \). 
C. The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 12 \). 
D. The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 10 \).
normalized_answer_text: The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 10 \).

normalized_choice_map.A: The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 8 \).
normalized_choice_map.B: The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 10 \).
normalized_choice_map.C: The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{2 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 12 \).
normalized_choice_map.D: The amplitude of the system's output \( c(t) \) is \( A = \frac{4 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \) and the frequency is \( \omega = 10 \).

normalized_question_text: Question: In the stable closed-loop system shown in , the asymptotic Bode magnitude plot of \( G(s) \) has a constant slope of \(-20 \, \text{dB/decade}\) at least till \( 100 \, \text{rad/sec} \) with the gain crossover frequency being \( 10 \, \text{rad/sec} \). The asymptotic Bode phase plot remains constant at \(-90^\circ\) at least till \( \omega = 10 \, \text{rad/sec} \). The steady-state error of the closed-loop system for a unit ramp input is _____ (rounded off to 2 decimal places).

Question: The logical relationship of variables \( A \), \( B \), and function \( F \) is as shown in figure, and its logical expression is:
Choices
A. \( F = AB \)
B. \( F = A + B \)
C. \( F = \overline{AB} \)
D. \( F = A \oplus B \)

Question: In the amplifier circuit shown in the figure, the transistor has a β = 30, \( U_{BE} = 0.7V \), and \( C_1 \), \( C_2 \), and \( C_E \) are sufficiently large. When the input voltage \( u_i = 0.141 \sin \omega t V \), what is the output voltage \( u_o \) = ( )\sin \omega t V?
